title: [6] SWIR Bio Imaging System (2026 - Present)
summary: This project concentrated on pioneering applications, where visible imaging fails. I focused on creating specialized tools due to limited existing research.

I developed custom SWIR systems: cameras, microscopes, and spectroscopy. I successfully built a SWIR microscope to non-destructively observe silicon transparency phenomena. Optimizing the optical design, the system achieved a double SNR compared to conventional instruments.
My roles included application planning, optical design/assembly, characterization, and unconventional data analysis.
We are currently studying on hyperspectral imaging with coded aperture.

title: [5] SWIR Image Sensors based on Quantum Dot (2024 - Present)
summary: This project focuses on developing monolithic image sensors using the PbS QDs(quantum dots) for SWIR(Short-Wavelength InfraRed).
Through optoelectronic device modelling, we achieved 10% EQE with a 200nA/cm2 dark current density.
My primary roles include device architecture design, optoelectronic evaluation, property analysis guiding subsequent design stage
We are currently preparing to commercialize this image sensor.

title: [4] Direct X-Ray Detectors based on Quantum Dot (2023 - 2025)
summary: This project focuses on developing monolithic image sensors using the PbS QDs(quantum dots) for X-Ray mammographic detectors
Through optoelectronic device modelling, we achieved 40µC/(Gy cm2) sensitivity with 5nA/cm2 dark current density for X-Ray.

My primary roles include device architecture design, optoelectronic evaluation, property analysis guiding subsequent design stages. We are currently investigating the QD size effect on X-Ray detection performance.

title: [3] Metasurface for High Spatial Resolution indirect X-Ray Detectors (2023 - Present)
summary: This project addressed the fundamental limitation in spatial resolution of indirect X-Ray detectors caused by isotropic radiation within scintillators.

I proposed and developed a solution embedding metasurfaces between the scintillators and image sensors to compensate and focus the light. This novel approach resulted in quadruple enhancement of MTF from 0.15 to 0.6.
My roles included conceptualizing the idea, metasurface design, optical simulation, and MTF calculation. Currently, we investigate simplified metasurfaces via inverse design.

title: [2] Surface Enhanced Raman Scattering for Point of Care Testing (2021 - 2023)
summary: This project focused on developing a Laser Scanning Raman spectroscope to quantitatively measure SERS from Au nanoparticles and Raman indicators in fluid.

Leveraging nanoparticle physics, I designed and developed an instrument specifically to enhance reproducibility. By implementing spatiotemporal ensemble averaging and outlier sorting, I reduced the SERS intensity coefficients of variation from 40% to 15%.
My primary roles involved understanding physics, designing and developing optical instruments, and data analysis via custom algorithms.

title: [1] Graphene based Optoelectronic Device (2016 - 2018)
summary: This project empirically demonstrated photodoping effect in a graphene-perovskite heterostructure.

Photocurrent measurements estimated the photodoping level to be approximately 3E+12/cm2 at zero gate bias, corresponding to Fermi energy variation from 0.37 to 0.43eV. This is sufficient to operate graphene-based active photonic devices at mid-infrared frequencies, providing an alternative to electrostatic gating.
My primary roles included device design and fabrication, optoelectronic evaluation, and property analysis applied to subsequent design stages.
